title: GraphNode Object
description: An individual node within a graph.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the graphNode and all of its connections.

methods.name: getInputPinCount
methods.description: How many input pins does this node have.

methods.name: getInputPinName
methods.description: The name of this graph node input pin describing its function.

methods.name: getInputPinType
methods.description: Get the type of the node input pin.

methods.name: getOutputPinCount
methods.description: How many output pins does this node have.

methods.name: getOutputPinName
methods.description: The name of this graph node input pin describing its function.

methods.name: getOutputPinType
methods.description: Get the type of the node output pin.

methods.name: hasValidProperties
methods.description: Check if the graph node properties are valid.

methods.name: isInputPinOptional
methods.description: Some input pins can be optional, so they do not need to be connected for the node to work.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A user readable description that explains the function of this node type.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of this graph node as give on creation. Node names for each graph should be unique.

properties.name: nodeType
properties.description: Get the string node type that is was created with.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Get a collection of all node properties supported by this node.

related_samples.name: Volumetric Custom Feature API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to create a Volumetric Custom Feature using the API for graph creation.

To run the sample script, have a document open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will create a component with a box by sketching then extruding that sketch. It will then use that box as a boundary body and create a Volumetric Custom Feature.

The script will the create a gyroid lattice using the Volumetric Model API with the appropriate Graphs, Nodes and Connections for a minimal example. Finally, the script will convert that Volumetric Model to Mesh using the API and the VolumetricModelToMeshFeature.

classType.title: GraphNode.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: GraphNode.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the graphNode and all of its connections.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true in the case where the deletion was successful. All properties and proery objects of this node will become invalid after this call. Output nodes cannot be deleted.

getInputPinCount.title: GraphNode.getInputPinCount Method
getInputPinCount.description: How many input pins does this node have.

getInputPinName.title: GraphNode.getInputPinName Method
getInputPinName.description: The name of this graph node input pin describing its function.

parameters.name: pinIndex
parameters.description: Zero based index of the pin to get. Should be less than the pin count.

getInputPinType.title: GraphNode.getInputPinType Method
getInputPinType.description: Get the type of the node input pin.

parameters.name: pinIndex
parameters.description: Zero based index of the pin to get. Should be less than the pin count.

getOutputPinCount.title: GraphNode.getOutputPinCount Method
getOutputPinCount.description: How many output pins does this node have.

getOutputPinName.title: GraphNode.getOutputPinName Method
getOutputPinName.description: The name of this graph node input pin describing its function.

parameters.name: pinIndex
parameters.description: Zero based index of the pin to get. Should be less than the pin count.

getOutputPinType.title: GraphNode.getOutputPinType Method
getOutputPinType.description: Get the type of the node output pin.

parameters.name: pinIndex
parameters.description: Zero based index of the pin to get. Should be less than the pin count.

hasValidProperties.title: GraphNode.hasValidProperties Method
hasValidProperties.description: Check if the graph node properties are valid.
hasValidProperties.return_description: True if the node has good inputs for its properties, false otherwise.

isInputPinOptional.title: GraphNode.isInputPinOptional Method
isInputPinOptional.description: Some input pins can be optional, so they do not need to be connected for the node to work.
isInputPinOptional.return_description: True if pin is optional, false if it is required.

parameters.name: pinIndex
parameters.description: Zero based index of the pin to get. Should be less than the pin count.

description.title: GraphNode.description Property
description.description: A user readable description that explains the function of this node type.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = graphNode_var.description

isValid.title: GraphNode.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = graphNode_var.isValid

name.title: GraphNode.name Property
name.description: The name of this graph node as give on creation. Node names for each graph should be unique.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = graphNode_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
graphNode_var.name = propertyValue

nodeType.title: GraphNode.nodeType Property
nodeType.description: Get the string node type that is was created with.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = graphNode_var.nodeType

objectType.title: GraphNode.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = graphNode_var.objectType

properties.title: GraphNode.properties Property
properties.description: Get a collection of all node properties supported by this node.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = graphNode_var.properties

related_samples.name: Volumetric Custom Feature API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to create a Volumetric Custom Feature using the API for graph creation.

To run the sample script, have a document open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will create a component with a box by sketching then extruding that sketch. It will then use that box as a boundary body and create a Volumetric Custom Feature.

The script will the create a gyroid lattice using the Volumetric Model API with the appropriate Graphs, Nodes and Connections for a minimal example. Finally, the script will convert that Volumetric Model to Mesh using the API and the VolumetricModelToMeshFeature.
